mechanism: Azure Policy denies resource types not on the platform-approved list at the management group level. The list is a curated parameter file versioned with the platform.
why: The whitelist is the platform's approval gate — a resource type absent from it has no policy coverage or diagnostic settings. Security and observability coverage begins when the type is added.

related.note: Must use an Allowed-prefixed assignment for resource-type compliance.
Every approved type must have a diagnostic settings definition — the two lists stay in sync.
New resource type triggers review of whether function-scoped roles need new actions.
Self-service exemptions make the hard deny viable.
New resource type with no built-in Deny policy requires a custom definition before security coverage is complete.

violations: Resource type not in allowedResources.json deployed via an unjustified exemption.
